**Your immediate priority**: generate revenue before payroll drains your runway.
Complete these steps now (multiple commands per turn are fine):

4. `yc-bench task dispatch --task-id Task-42` — start work
5. `yc-bench sim resume` — advance time

**IMPORTANT**: Check each command's result before proceeding to the next.
If `task accept` fails (trust or prestige too low), try a different task.
Do NOT call `sim resume` unless you have at least one active task — it will skip forward with zero revenue.

time_series.scratchpad:
Core rules: always keep at least one active task before sim resume. Prefer clients/messages indicating established process, clear scope, reliable payment. Be careful with salary bumps: avoid assigning unnecessary employees; use best-fit small teams by domain. Trust concentration matters, but at start prioritize reliable clients and near-certain wins to build prestige and cash.
Observed from early failures: initial teams were too small for task volumes/deadlines. Need inspect before assigning; estimate throughput vs deadline and overstaff enough to finish early, even if salary bumps rise. Prioritize reliable-scope clients (Helix/Cortex style messaging) and shorter/clearer tasks.
Throughput math matters: 4-person team (Emp4,7,2,8) completed 581 inference in under ~6 days; 3-person inference team missed 1294 by ~27h; 2-person data team badly missed 1201. Use rough daily capacity = sum rates*24 and insist on strong margin before deadline. Consider overlapping only with idle employees to avoid split penalties.
Client strategy update: Cortex trust now >2 and successful twice; prioritize Cortex clear-scope tasks, including trust-gated ones. Helix still has 100% fail history from us; only take Helix again with ample deadline margin and proper staffing.
Update: Helix can succeed with reliable-scope tasks when properly staffed; prior failure was sizing, not necessarily client unreliability. Still prefer clear-scope clients and adequate margin.
Research staffing lesson reinforced: 3-person team Emp6+Emp1+Emp3 was insufficient for 1038 research over ~9 days, finishing ~19h late. For large research tasks, use stronger team including Emp4/7/8/2 as needed; do not assume idle weaker specialists are enough.
Failure update: 5-person research team Emp4+7+8+2+6 still missed 2256 research in ~9 days by 118h while 3-person low-skill inference team Emp1+3+5 missed 740 inference in ~11 days by 29h. Recompute capacity carefully from actual progress; be much more conservative on large research tasks and never staff inference with weak employees only.
Critical lesson: assigning only Emp_1, Emp_3, Emp_5 to an 832 inference task was a major under-staffing mistake; observed progress was only 4.2271/832 after ~41 minutes sim time. For large inference tasks, never use weak trio alone; use strong inference employees Emp_2, Emp_4, Emp_7, Emp_8 unless deadline is extremely long.
Observed Task-119 research rate with only Emp_6: 658.4 progress over ~10.1 days (~65/day), confirming single-mid staffing is hopeless for 3440/11d research. When rescuing big research tasks late, immediately add Emp_4 plus Emp_7/8/2 as needed; do not leave large research jobs solo.
Failure update: 5-person research team Emp4+7+8+2+6 still missed 2256 research in ~9 days by 118h while 3-person low-skill inference team Emp1+3+5 missed 740 inference in ~11 days by 29h. Recompute capacity carefully from actual progress; be much more conservative on large research tasks and never staff inference with weak employees only.
Do not accept/rely on overdue rescue tasks like Equinox Task-101; once a task is already past deadline it will still fail on completion. Focus only on tasks that can finish before deadline. Also avoid single-employee data tasks unless required_qty/deadline margin clearly supports it.
Training staffing lesson: Task-99 with Emp_1+Emp_4+Emp_5 achieved only 589/1131 over ~5.77 days, so ~102/day; that team is insufficient for 1131 due in 11 days if not started immediately, and risky when overlapping decisions reduce time margin. Be more conservative on training tasks; prefer Emp_6/7/8 support or smaller qty/longer deadlines.
Observed rescue pattern: when an active task shows low progress relative to remaining time, immediately add a top employee even if they are on another task. Example: Task-176 with only Emp_4 had 144/735 after ~2.98 days (~48/day), too slow for 11-day deadline; adding Emp_7 was necessary.
Avoid overcommitting top inference staff across too many tasks simultaneously; if taking a third concurrent inference task, only do so when weak trio capacity is clearly sufficient for qty/deadline, otherwise wait. Reinspect immediately after progress checkpoint.
Failure update: low-skill inference trio Emp_1+Emp_3+Emp_5 produced only 292/995 over ~3.1 days on Task-86 (~94/day), so such staffing is inadequate for ~1k inference due in ~8 days. Rescue immediately with Emp_4 and Emp_7; do not start similar jobs with weak trio.
Strong current inference staffing split: Emp2+8+6 can comfortably handle ~600-700 inference in ~11 days; Emp4+7 plus weak trio can also handle ~660 with large margin when not overlapping elsewhere. Use these as dual concurrent inference squads.
Reliable inference farming working well: alternating Cortex/Helix with two fixed squads yields repeated early completions. Helix trust now near 4 again; safe to keep taking clear-scope Helix inference tasks with adequate deadlines.
Inference farming update Jul-11: repeated dual-squad inference strategy still works extremely well. Current rates roughly Emp2+6+8 = 30/day-equivalent units/hour? Practical result: handles 600-1000 inference tasks in 2-5 days with huge margin. Emp4+7+1+3+5 also reliably clears ~600-900 inference tasks in similar window. Prefer Helix/Cortex inference; continue avoiding Equinox/Vanguard unless no reliable options.
Massive inference task warning: Equinox Task-179 had 6456 inference due in 14 days. Our standard 5-person secondary squad is far too small for that volume based on prior observed rates. Second Equinox inference warning: Task-65 required 5796 inference due in 13 days, also far beyond our capacity. Never accept Equinox inference tasks in the 5k-6k range; inspect before accepting and if already accepted, avoid dispatch if possible or treat as likely failure.
Failure confirmed Aug-22: Equinox Task-179 (6456/14d) and Task-65 (5796/13d) both failed massively late despite at-risk=false early. Treat simulator at-risk flag as unreliable for giant tasks; rely on our own throughput estimates only. Never take 5k+ inference jobs with current 8-person team.
